text: In political science, a "nation" refers to a group of people who feel bound into a single body by shared culture, values, folkways, religion and/or language.  A "state" just refers to a patch of land with a sovereign government.  States often coincide with nations (and are called "nation-states," but not always.  States that overlap multiple nations tend to have civil wars; states that exclude parts of a nation tend to have wars with the neighboring state(s) that contain the rest of the nation.

text: A cloud compsition is primarlly just billions of drops of water or ice. text: Compartment syndrome occurs when a fixed compartment, defined by myofascial elements or bone, becomes subject to increased pressure, leading to ischemia and organ dysfunction. Well recognized to occur in the extremities, it also occurs in the abdomen, and some believe, in the intracranial cavity. The exact clinical conditions that define abdominal compartment syndrome (ACS) are controversial; however, organ dysfunction caused by intra-abdominal hypertension (IAH) is considered to be ACS. The dysfunction may be respiratory insufficiency secondary to compromised tidal volumes, decreased urine output caused by falling renal perfusion, or any organ dysfunction associated with impaired perfusion.

Well recognized to occur in the extremities, it also occurs in the abdomen, and some believe, in the intracranial cavity. The exact clinical conditions that define abdominal compartment syndrome (ACS) are polemic; conversely, organ dysfunction caused by intra-abdominal hypertension (IAH) is considered to be ACS. The dysfunction may be respiratory insufficiency secondary to compromised tidal volumes, decreased urine output caused by falling renal perfusion, or any organ dysfunction associated with impaired perfusion.

text: Let's answer this by looking at goals. Large publicly held companies have one goal: to maximize shareholder value. text:
Cerium(IV) oxide, ceric oxide, ceria, or sometimes simply cerium oxide or cerium dioxide, is a pale yellow-white powder, CeO2. It is used in ceramics, to polish glass, and to sensitize photosensitive glass. It is also used in lapidary as "jeweller's rouge" and in the walls of self-cleaning ovens.

It is slightly hygroscopic and will also absorb a small amount of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.

Note that cerium also forms cerium(III) oxide, Ce2O3.

text: A pimple is a type of skin lesion caused by inflamed and/or obstructed pores. The most common cause of pimples is acne. In response, the skin inflames, producing the visible lesion. The face, chest, back, shoulders and upper arms are especially affected.

text:
Depending on the cost, it may be easiest to expense the uniforms.  This type of thing would probably be a depreciable asset rather than one you amortize.  

For depreciation, you'll need to follow IRS guidelines on depreciable property.  In a nutshell, you'll need to know 2 key things -- 'basis' and 'useful life'.

Basis is essentially the cost of the uniforms.  Useful life is how long the uniforms will be 'in service'.  To depreciate, you essentially do:
   basis / useful life = periodic depreciation expense.

text:
It's Spanish. "Hasta" means "until", so you can say "Hasta luego" (until later) or "Hasta mañana" (until tomorrow).

"la" is just a gender preposition, denoting that "vista" (view) is feminine ("el" is masculine).

The figurative meaning is "Until later" or "until we see each other again".

There are 8 bits in every byte. And 1024 bytes in every kilobyte. And finally there are 1024 kilobytes in every megabyte.

text:
There is light in space. After all, the light we see on Earth from the Sun, the moon, and the stars travels through space to get here. 

The big difference between Earth and space is that our atmosphere reflects the light that goes through it. So it makes our surroundings seem brighter. Since space doesn't hold an atmosphere there is nothing to reflect the light and it just passes through.

So basically light is always travelling through space but it doesn't get reflected (which makes it dark).

Hope that makes some sense.

text:
The similarities are
1. Both are unsaturated.
2. Both have pi bonds which overlap laterally.
3. Both are susceptible to electrophilic attack.
4. Bond length of both alkene and alkyne is less than that of alkane.

The differences are
1. Alkene has single pi bond whereas, alkyne has 2 pi bonds.
2. The C-C bond distance is less in alkyne compared to alkene.
3. Carbon atoms are sp2 hybridized in alkene. On the other hand, carbon atoms involved in the triple bond in alkyne is sp hybridized.

The answer is really quite simple:

When we speak of the 'age of the universe' we are talking about how old all the rest of the mass of the universe would appear from within _our_ frame of reference.

So, to a cosmic radiation particle traveling at near the speed of light the whole universe would "appear" to be just seconds or minutes old. (As if such a particle could have perception, which it cannot).

Yet we, all living on the same planet and having less than a thousand mile-per-hour difference in our respective velocities, are unable to notice the effect of Relativity.

text:
The sky appears blue due to what is known as the Rayleigh scattering effect

Note that Blue colour has shorter wavelength than red(wavelength is a physical quantity that characterises a particular colour)

The sun emits white light(contains all visible colours), but as the light travels through the atmosphere the shorter wavlengths gets absorbed by the air molecules while longer ones travel almost unaffected. 
 
The absorbed light is then re-emmited in all direction hence effectively gets scattered in the atmosphere. Since blue is the shortest it gets scattered most and whichever direction you look you see some blue colour.

text: It very much sounds like tinea pedis, or athlete's foot.  Nonprescription antifungals usually are used first. These include terbinafine (Lamisil AT), miconazole (Micatin), clotrimazole (Lotrimin AF), and tolnaftate (Tinactin, Tinaderm). \nPrescription antifungals are used if nonprescription medications are not successful or if you have a severe infection. These include naftifine (Naftin), butenafine (Mentax), miconazole (Monistat Derm), and clotrimazole (Lotrimin). Griseofulvin is a prescription medication that slows the growth of fungi. It is used to treat athlete's foot. If over the counter medication does not make it improve, please see your doctor.

text: I have cats and cat allergy and here is what I learned from a visit to an allergist.  There are four levels of overcoming an allergy: 1) get rid of the cat(s); 2) minimize the allergens by keeping the cat(s) out of your bedroom, using a HEPA air filter, washing the cat, and so on; 3) treat the symptoms with antihistamines or other prescription drugs that block the immunoresponse; 4) get periodic shots of cat allergen which, over time, will diminish your immunoresponse to the allergen.

text:
Non-profit does not = not making money.  It also does not mean volunteer.  A non-profit corporation plows all the money it makes beyond salaries and operating expense either back into the business or distributes the money for a charitable purpose.

A non-profit is defined by its tax status, not by whether or not it brings in a lot of money.  The Red Cross, for example, has many many local branches and brings in millions.  But it is non-profit. 

A properly-organized credit union is tax-exempt under 501(c)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code.

text:
The Electoral College is a group of electors who vote for the president.  The group members for each state are all the state's US House and Senate members.  There are a total of 538 members in the Electoral College.  All electors from a state must vote for the majority, although it wasn't always this way.

The popular vote is simply the measure of how people voted in the entire United States.  This does not choose the President, the Electoral College does.

Hope this makes sense!  Use the information that you need.

The javax.servlet.Servlet interface defines the three methods known as life-cycle method.
public void init(ServletConfig config) throws ServletException
public void service( ServletRequest req, ServletResponse res) throws ServletException, IOException
public void destroy()
First the servlet is constructed, then initialized wih the init() method.
Any request from client are handled initially by the service() method before delegating to the doXxx() methods in the case of HttpServlet.

The servlet is removed from service, destroyed with the destroy() methid, then garbaged collected and finalized.

text: Question: If nothing sticks to Teflon® then how does it stick to a pan?\n\nAnswer: Non-sticks are applied in layers, just like paint. The first layer is the primer and it is the special chemistry in the primer that makes it adhere to the metal surface of the pan. The primer will adhere either to a substrate that is prepared by grit blasting or roughening the surface, or it can be applied over a smooth substrate.

text:
Snow is made of small crystals of ice that have a different refractive index due to the air between them. When light passes across an interface between
two media with different refractive indices, some of it reflect off the interface, and some is transmitted at an angle.  Every transit causes a
scattering of the light, so that eventually light falling the sample is returned in every direction. When all the light is reflected naturally it will be white. 
Ice on the other hand lets the light through making it colorless - it is a single crystal!
adv_text:

two media with different refractive indices, some of it reflect off the interface, and some is transmitted at an angle.  Every transit causes a
scattering of the light, so that eventually light falling the sample is returned in every direction. Everytime all the light is reflected naturally it will be white. 
Ice on the alia hand lets the light through making it colorless - it is a single crystal!

text:
While I doubt that there is a single hormone that causes "selfishness", selfish behaviour is a survival trait giving an edge over competitors.

Selfishness accordingly is found not only in humans but in all other life forms, too.

Selfishness alone is not a good survival trait because taken too far it destroys the basis of survival.

Social systems transfer selfishness of the individual to selfishness of the community. Often this strategy has proved superior to individual selfishness, even though some individuals will not profit.

text:
If you're asking: "How can I set MSIE up so that when I open a new MSIE window, it opens as Maximized?"

Try this.  When you open a new window move it and "stretch it" to fill the entire screen (do NOT use the Max button or double click the title bar... you must do this manually), then hold down the Alt key and close the window by clicking on the X.

MSIE will now remember this size when new windows are opened.

text:
"Congressmen" is another term for members of the U.S. House of Representatives.  All representatives are elected for 2-year terms, with elections held in November during the even-number years.

U.S. senators, on the other hand, are elected to 6-year terms.  The terms are staggered so that every 2 years, roughly a third of the senate seats have elections.  These elections are held at the same time as the congressional elections.

Sometimes if someone resigns in the middle of a term, there will be a special election to replace him/her.  Those special elections can be any time and in any year.
adv_text:

text:
According to the lorentz transformation the space time coordinate of two inertial reference frame moving at a relative speed v along x axis is
x'=(x-v*t)/L
y'=y 
z'=z
t'=(t-v*x/c^2)
where L is lorentz factor=sqrt(1/(1-v^2/c^2));

The tricky thhing is this if any object moves faster than light the L of above equation will become imaginary.
So for an observer the proper time t' and the relativistic mass m=Lm0 becomes imaginary...
 Thus it cannot be faster than light.
However theoritically there exist tachyon particles which moves in superluminal(faster than light)  velocity with imaginary proper time.
 But those crazy physix!!!

text: We will likely achieve speeds arbitrarily close to light speed.  Mass (rest mass) does not increase with speed, so the argument about our biology being able to handle a mass increase is faulty. At a constant acceleration of 10g (ten times the earth's gravity, the approximate human limit) it would take about a year to achieve a speed close to light.

text: The origin of the term is disputed. text:
let x=velocity of water downward and y=velocity of salmon. since the salmon travels 24km downstream in 3hours, the velocity of the salmon is:

24km/3hr=y+x (in this case, both the water and salmon are travelling downwards)

similarly, the salmon travels upstream in 5hours:

24km/5hr=y-x(in this case, the salmon is travelling upstream while the water is travelling downwards, thus "-x")

add the two equations and you get:

24km/3hr + 24km/5hr = 2y

y = (120km/15hr + 72km/15hr)/2

y = (192km/15hr)/2

y = 32km/5hr

thus the salmon will swim at 32km/5hr in still water.  this translates to 16m/9s in case you wanted to know :)

text: Assuming you're asking about a plain ol' atomic bomb, the process at its heart is called fission. At the core of such a bomb is a relatively unstable material, like uranium. Surrounding this core is a shaped explosive charge. When this charge is set off, the core material undergoes tremendous compression, increasing its density to the point that individual sub-atomic particles (neutrons) ram together which releases more particles which ram together, which releases more particles, and so on. Each particle collision releases energy --- nuclear fission has occurred and the bomb detonates.

text:
Hmm...not sure if this question is answerable.  Not sure where the term "annual total return" comes from.  Annual compound return is really only useful if you're talking about periods over more than a year.  Assuming that you mean to compare "total % return" and annual compound rate, you can use:

Total % Return = (1 + (Annual % Rate))^(Years)

Assume a 5% annual compound return, then after 10 years you would have

(1 + .05)^10 = 1.62

You would have 162% of what you started with, so if you started with $1, you would have $1.62.

text:
1 mol Zn = 65.39 g
So 142.5 g of Zn is equal to 2.18 moles.

From the reaction you can tell that your final product is two moles Cl for every mole of Zn. So your final product must contain 2.18 moles Zn and 4.36 moles Cl (2.18x2).

1 mol Cl = 35.45 g
So 4.36 moles Cl is equal to 154.6 g of Cl

So the theoretical yield is:
142.5 g + 154.6 g = 297.1 g

You can put that in scientific notation yourself.

text:
Equity Market
The market in which shares are issued and traded, either through exchanges or over-the-counter markets. Also known as the stock market, it is one of the most vital areas of a market economy because it gives companies access to capital and investors a slice of ownership in a company with the potential to realize gains based on its future performance.

Investopedia Says: This market can be split into two main sectors: the primary and secondary market. The primary market is where new issues are first offered. Any subsequent trading takes place in the secondary market.

3.  Sleep with your head elevated. 

Calcium may also help by reducing stomach acidity.  (Tums is just calcium carbonate.)

The following expenses may be tax-deductible, depending on the particulars of your tax situation. Buying a home is a great time to visit a tax professional, just to make sure you are keeping track of all your deductible expenses.

* Pre-paid mortgage interest (also called "points") shown on your closing statement.
* Pre-paid real estate taxes shown on your closing statement.
* Regularly paid mortgage interest (reported to you by your bank on Form 1098).
* Regularly paid real estate taxes (reported to you on the tax bill from your County's Assessor).

William Perez
taxes.about.com

text: The Electoral College is a controversial mechanism of presidential elections that was created by the framers of the U.S. Constitution as a compromise for the presidential election process. At the time, some politicians believed a purely popular election was too reckless, while others objected to giving Congress the power to select the president. The compromise was to set up an Electoral College system that allowed voters to vote for electors, who would then cast their votes for candidates, a system described in Article II, section 1 of the Constitution.

We know Q = mass x specific heat x increase in temp.
          = m x s x (dt)

Q(A)/Q(B) = (m(A) x s(A) x (dt)(A))/(m(B) x s(B) x (dt)(B))
Given, Q(A) = Q(B) as they absorb same amount of energy
       m(A) = m(B), dt(A) = 8, dt(B) = 16

Putting values we get s(A) = 2 x s(B)

So 1. F 2. T 3. T 4. F 5. F

text: The Republic of Iraq sits on land that is historically known as Mesopotamia (Assyrian: Bet Nahrain), which means 'land between the rivers' in Greek. This land was home to some of the world's first and most distinguished civilizations. These included Sumerian, Akkadian, Babylonian, Assyrian and many other cultures, whose influence extended into neighbouring regions, certainly from around 5000 BC. These civilizations produced some of the first writing, science, mathematics, law and philosophy known to man, making it the center of what is commonly called the "Cradle of Civilization". Ancient Mesopotamian civilization dominated other civilizations of its time.

text: There is no specific right to privacy granted in the constitution. Rather, the Supreme Court has ruled that it is implicit in the "penumbras" of the various amendments. text: An automobile has a DC (direct current) battery and therefore uses a DC starter.  Current flows in only one direction just as in a flashlight with batteries.\n\nMany snowblowers can be plugged into an AC (alternating current) outlet in the garage and use AC starters.  Current flows in opposite directions through the starter completing 60 cycles every second.  The voltage rises from zero to a peak drops to zero and continues to a negative peak before returning back to zero once each cycle.  The graph of voltage with respect to time forms a sinusoid.

text: Krypton is a chemical element with the symbol Kr and atomic number 36. A colorless noble gas, krypton occurs in trace amounts in the atmosphere, is isolated by fractionating liquefied air, and is often used with other rare gases in fluorescent lamps. Krypton can also form clathrates with water when atoms of it are trapped in a lattice of the water molecules.

text: A thermoplastic is a material that is plastic or deformable, melts to a liquid when heated, and freezes to a brittle, glassy state when cooled sufficiently. Most thermoplastics are high molecular weight polymers whose chains associate through weak van der Waals forces (polyethylene); stronger dipole-dipole interactions and hydrogen bonding (nylon); or even stacking of aromatic rings (polystyrene). Thermoplastic polymers are contrasted with thermosetting polymers (Bakelite; vulcanized rubber), which, once formed and cured, can never be remelted and remolded. Many thermoplastic materials are addition polymers; e.g., vinyl chain-growth polymers, such as polyethylene and polypropylene.

text: MVC is an acronym for Model->View->Controller. It is a design pattern that allows models (data) to be seperated from the views (the user interface) by letting a controller mediate all the interactions between them. The controller can be used to access any model to manipulate data and then letting the user interface to display it. The user interface does not have any bindings with the model (data) directly and it therefore transparent (protected in some sense) to any changes to the model.

text:
Like is a feeling, and love is an action.
Love is a decision that you make.  You decide to love someone even when they aren't lovely, like first thing in the morning, or when you are in an arguement.
When you love someone, it isn't contingent on how they make you feel, or what they do for you.  Love is unselfish, and it means you put someone else's feelings before your own.
Love isn't something you "fall" in, and out of.  You either never loved them in the first place, or you decided not to work at loving that person anymore.

text: To clarify the previous answer, the picture of the klein bottle is not really what a klein bottle "looks like." The mobeus strip is a 2-d surface, but you can only visualize it in 3 dimensions. Similarly, the klein bottle is a 3-d surface, but you can only visualize it in 4 spatial dimensions. Since humans can't visualize in 4 spatial dimensions, they make a 3d representation of it. Because the representation is in 3d, the klein bottle looks like it is piercing itself. In 4 dimensions it does no such thing.

Hi. 

The quantity of heat evolved is just the product of the temperature change times the heat capacity 

-(5.510KJ/1C)3.51C = -19.34 KJ 

Since this is the amount of heat that results from combustion of 1.00g of hydrazine, the amount released by combustion of 1 mol of hydrazine is 
(-19.34KJ/1 g hydrazine)(32.0 g hydrazine/1 mol hydrazine) = -619 KJ/mol hydrazine 

I hope this helps!

--Stacey

text: School isn't more difficult, but today's students lead more complex lives, therefore life in general is more difficult. Since the answer needs to be in cm^3, work in centimeters from the start.  31.7mm = 3.17cm

Circumference = 2*pi*r
so 
r = C/(2*pi)
r = 3.17cm/(2*pi)
r = 0.50cm

Volume = (4/3)*pi*r^3 
V = (4/3)*pi*(0.50cm)^3
V = 0.52cm^3  

Density = Mass/Volume 
D = 4.2g/0.52cm^3 
D = 8.07g/cm^3

Good call chella.  Above reflects corrections.

RS-232: intended for data rate less than 20 kbps
RS-422: similar to RS-232 but uses differential signalling.
RS-485:  descendant of RS-422, which can be use for a bus or multi-drop.

text: ESD is Electro-Static Discharge. Since I/O's on chips are the only thing electrically connected to the external world, ESD events would affect them the most. This can happen during packaging (putting the die in the package) or when attaching the package to the board. To protect against ESD overloading and damaging the I/O's, the pads are designed with diodes in them "pointing" from ground to the signal wire. Normally no current flows through the diodes, but when a high ESD voltage is introduced, then current flows through the diode to ground, protecting the sensitive circuit.

This can happen during packaging (putting the die in the package) or when attaching the package to the board. To protect against ESD overloading and damaging the I/O's, the pads are designed with diodes in them "pointing" from ground to the signal wire. text:
blind spot


Anatomy. The small, circular, optically insensitive region in the retina where fibers of the optic nerve emerge from the eyeball. It has no rods or cones. Also called optic disk.
A part of an area that cannot be directly observed under existing circumstances.
An area where radio reception is weak or nonexistent.
A subject about which one is markedly ignorant or prejudiced: “Of course, all of us . . . have our crotchets and our blind spots. I abhor novels written in the present tense” (Mordecai Richler).

text:
Intangible assets are defined as assets that are not physical in nature. Common examples of intangible assets include intellectual property rights, such as copyrights, patents, trademarks, and trade secrets (e.g., customer lists and know-how), as well as goodwill.

The Uniform Commercial Code (Section 9-102(a)(42)) defines "general intangibles" as

"any personal property...other than accounts, chattel paper, commercial tort claims, deposit accounts, documents, goods, instruments, investment property, letter of credit rights, letters of credit, money, and oil, gas, or other minerals before extraction. The term includes payment intangibles and software."

The Gentleman's Magazine, first published in 1731, is considered to be the first general-interest magazine. The oldest magazine still in print is The Scots Magazine, which was first published in 1739, though multiple changes in ownership and gaps in publication totaling over 90 years weaken that claim.

The most widely distributed magazine in the world is The Watchtower Announcing Jehovah's Kingdom (founded in 1879). Its worldwide circulation including all editions comprises 26.5 million copies.

text:
Red cabbage contains a pigment molecule called flavin, an anthocyanin. 

Acidic solutions turn anthocyanin a red color. Neutral solutions produce a purplish color and basic solutions produce greenish-yellow.

The color of the red cabbage juice changes because of changes in its hydrogen ion concentration. Acids donate hydrogen ions and have a low pH ( < 7). Bases accept hydrogen ions and have a high pH ( > 7).

Note: pH is the -log[H+].

text: Paddle shift. There are 2 paddles behind the wheel, one on the right, one on the left. Right is upshift, left is downshift. You pretty much just tap it by doing a squeeze/inward motion with your fingers. And they don't need to use a clutch, it's a sequential gearbox so instead of shifting to a gear (like 4th straight to 1st), you shift 4th > 3rd > 2nd > 1st in order. But thanks to their superlight clutchpads and flywheels, shifts are super quick, so you can shift rapidfire.

text:
Archimedes' principle states that the buoyant force experienced by a submerged object is equal to the weight of the liquid displaced by the object. Experimentally this appears in the fact that the submerged object apparently weighs less by an amount equal to the weight of the liquid displaced. The buoyant force can be expressed as:

Fb = W(air) - W(liquid) = dgv 

where d is the density of the liquid, g is the acceleration of gravity and v is the volume of the immersed object (or the immersed part of the body if it floats).

text: The Production Possibilities Frontier describes possible allocations of resources AT efficient production levels. text: Sugars are chains of carbon with -OH (alcohol) groups hanging off each carbon.  When two sugars join, the -OH groups of two sugars merge. The sugars as then joined by carbons being linked to the same O, also called an ether bond.

text:
Under the ptolemaic model, you had to come up with a very complex orbit for the planets to make it fit within the concepts of what we understood as motion (remember this was also pre-Newtonian mechanics).

Under the Copernical model, it was very simple but also troubling, that all bodies orbited the sun, except for the moon which troubled again because it didn't.

The final nail in the ptolemaic model was the discovery by Gallileo of the moons of Jupiter, which dashed that theory and extended Copernicus's model, which was finally all put together with Newtonian mechanics.

text:
If it's an identity, then you aren't allowed to move terms from one side to the other. Instead you must manipulate one side (leaving the other untouched) until it is identical to the other.

Since the right side only depends on sinx (it has no cosx's), a good strategy would be to replace the cosx's on the left side.

We know (sinx)^2 + (cosx)^2 = 1, so
(cosx)^2 = 1 - (sinx)^2

Substitute this for (cosx)^2 on the left side, then simplify by combining like terms. Voila - you'll have the right side.

text:
You should perform a variety of exercises to strengthen or 'tone' your stomach muscles. This includes, incline sit-ups, crunches on a fitness ball, leg raises, etc.

One of the best ways to tone your stomach though is to perform weight lifting exercies that you do while standing or end up standing while holding the weight. This includes squats, deadlifts, standing shoulder presses, lunges, etc. The exercies require you to contract your abs to support the weight and can provide better benefits than crunches and sit-ups.

Curt

text: So far science doesn't know what so-called dark matter is. Dark matter is the name for some exotic type of mass that is required to explain the motions of large structures in space, like galaxies, clusters of galaxies, etc.,. At this time, we think that dark matter accounts for about 23% of the universe's mass, while regular matter can only account for about 4%. The remaining 73% is thought to be what's called 'dark energy,' the totally unknown feature that is causing the universe's expansion rate to speed up.

text:
Weight is a force.  The National Institute of Science and Technologiy defines it here as such.
http://physics.nist.gov/Pubs/SP811/sec08.html

A force is acceleration times mass.  Weight is the accleration due to gravity times mass.  On the Earth's surface, this is approximately:

Weight = Mass x 9.8 meters/second^2

For a accurate conversion, you would need to use the acceleration due gravity minus the accelration due to centrifugal force at your exact location.  

This is why physicists more commonly use mass than weight to describe an object.

Weight = Mass x 9.8 meters/second^2

For a accurate conversion, you would need to use the acceleration due gravity minus the accelration due to centrifugal force at your exact location.  

This is why physicists more commonly use mass than weight to describe an object.

text: The non-living cell wall surrounds the living protoplast. Within the cell wall the cell membrane encloses the cytoplasm containing organelles (chloroplasts, mitochondria, nucleus, etc.) and a vacuole.\nThe vacuole contains many chemicals dissolved in water. Osmotic pressure within the vacuole squashes the cytoplasm against the cell wall when the cell is turgid to provide shape and support.\nThe two major compartments of the living part of a plant cell are the cytoplasm and the vacuole.
adv_text: The non-living cell wall surrounds the living protoplast. Within the cell wall the cell membrane encloses the cytoplasm containing organelles (chloroplasts, mitochondria, nucleus, etc.) and a vacuole.\nThe vacuole contains many chemicals dissolved in water. Osmotic pressure within the vacuole squashes the cytoplasm against the cell wall when the cell is turgid to provide shape and support.\nThe two considerable closets of the living part of a plant cell are the cytoplasm and the vacuole.

text:
1.8GB Capacity, you might mean, or 1.8 GHz clock speed, more likely.

In either case, a 155MB (sure it's not 256MB?) may work.  It's not the memory that's the issue, ie, the numbers you mentioned don't have anything to do with compatibility.

Compatibility is addressed by checking videocard connection type: PCI, PCI-Express (also called PCI-E), and AGP.  Note that AGP comes in different flavors such as 2x and 4x.  Some motherboards do 2x or 4x and some do both.

I miss films that aren't filled to the brim with cgi.  To me, most of the computer effects look too fake.  I prefer well-made models and costumes (things that look like I could actually reach out and touch them) to cgi. Cgi makes high budget films look like cheap video games.
I thought all of the characters and ships, etc in the original Star Wars' and the dinosaur models in Jurassic Park were pretty believable.

I must admit, I don't mind studios using computers for adding multitudes of extras or for backdrops (but, I do wish more films were shot on location.)

text:
f(x) = x^2 - 1
If you replace x with x + h, then
f(x + h) = (x + h)^2 - 1
You will NOT do any operations when changing the function. Instead, just SUBSTITUTE. (A technique is putting parentheses in the variable to change, except if it already has its own parentheses. Then you may exchange the variables inside the parentheses with what you want to substitute)
f(x) = x^2 - 1
Putting the parentheses, we get
f(x) = (x)^2 - 1
Then replace x with x + h
f(x + h) = (x + h)^2 - 1
Hope this helps ;-)

text:
The term hemorrhoids refers to a condition in which the veins around the anus or lower rectum are swollen and inflamed.

Hemorrhoids may result from straining to move stool. Other contributing factors include pregnancy, aging, chronic constipation or diarrhea, and anal intercourse.

Hemorrhoids are either inside the anus (internal) or under the skin around the anus (external).

Many anorectal problems, including fissures, fistulae, abscesses, or irritation and itching (pruritus ani), have similar symptoms and are incorrectly referred to as hemorrhoids.

Latent heat of ice is 334kJ/kg.
This means, to convert 1kg of ice at 0c to 1kg of water at 0c it would require 334kJ (or 334000 Joules). 
So, to convert 0.3kg of ice to water at the same temperature would require 0.3*334 or about 100kJ.
The answer would be (A) above.

text: Our universe consists of four dimensions: three of space and one of time.  This leads many people to think that the "fourth dimension" refers to time, and higher dimensions must be associated with something else.  That isn't true.\n\nTheoretically, there is no limit to the number of possible dimensions, whether spatial or otherwise.  Another universe might have six dimensional space and two dimensional time, for instance.  We can't imagine such a place, but using math, it's pretty easy to describe its properties.

text:
The slope intercept form is y=mx+b. Basically, you solve for y. For your formula, you would do the following.

6x + 9y = 27   (Simplify by dividing by 3)
2x + 3y = 9    (Move x to right side of equation)
2x - 2x + 3y = -2x + 9 (Simplify)
3y = -2x + 9   ( Divide by 3 to solve for y)
3y/3 = (-2/3)x + 9/3 (Simplify)
y = (-2/3)x + 3

The (-2/3) is a negative two thirds. I just couldn't put that in this little box.

text: A composite number n is a positive integer 1 which is not prime (i.e., which has factors other than 1 and itself). The first few composite numbers (sometimes called "composites" for short) are 4, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16, ... (Sloane's A002808), which can be written 2^2, 2.3, 2^3, 3^2, 2.5, 2^2.3, 2.7, 3.5, and 2^4, respectively. The number 1 is a special case which is considered to be neither composite nor prime.

Primary sources are original records created at the time of a historical event.  Secondary sources are written after-the-fact, and they are usually commentary on the primary sources.  So, your bibliography of Mozart, for example, is a secondary source, though it will surely list many primary sources in its bibliography.

Probably you are like me in that genetically you have thin gum tissue.

text: Often times, when people see me drinking a cup of coffee -- they give the warning "You shouldn't drink coffee, it will give you ulcers." The thinking, until recently, was that excess stomach acid caused ulcers and that coffee would contribute to the stomach acid. Recent studies however show that most ulcers are caused by a particular bacteria, namely Helicobacter pylori . Those ulcers can be cured easily with antibiotics. An important distinction to make is that while coffee or spicy foods for that matter don't cause the ulcers, they may serve to aggravate existing ulcers.

text:
Knowing the basics of managing money.  Being financial literate means you know enough to be able to get yourself in good financial shape.  It does not guarantee it will happen, but it means you have a chance.

I would include the following in my definition of financial literacy:
Being able to put together a household budget.
Understanding interest rates and fees to a degree you can shop around for loans and investments.
A basic understanding of how a credit score works - paying bills on time and not running up too many debts.
Openning a checking and hopefully a savings account.

This is due to a property of log which is that :
log(ab)=log(a)+log(b)
Remark that this is true when well defined (for eg you can't write log(-1*-3)=log(-1)+log(-3) )

So here, you have log(x^2)=log(x)+log(x)

Therefore, there are no diff. except that log(x^2) exists for all x in R\{0} and 2log(x) is defined for all x strictly positive.

text: Ciprofloxacin is part of a class of antibiotics called the fluoroquinolones.  Side effects involving the CNS or central nervous system (brain/spinal cord) are always a possibility with this class of medications.  There are several prescription medications that can cause seizures by lowering the "seizure threshold".  This means that if you were going to have a seizure anyway, it would take less stimuli to cause it to happen.  Most people don't routinely experience seizures from ciprofloxacin.  However, if I had epilepsy (a seizure disorder), I would be careful with using ciprofloxacin, especially if my seizure disorder is poorly controlled.

text:
Directly, there is no solution.  If you use a series expansion around x=0, 
sin(x)=sum((-1)^n(x^(2n+1))/(factorial(2n+1)))
Divide this series by x, then integrate.  After the 3rd term, the error term goes to the ten millionths digit, so I'd say you're pretty safe :)

sin(x)/x = 1 - (x^2)/6 + (x^4)/120 - (x^6)/5040 + .....
Integrated is:  x - (x^3)/18 + (x^5)/600 - (x^7)/32580 +
or 
sum((-1)^n)(x^(2n+1))/(2n+1)*factorial(2n+1))

text:
Because Christianity founded itself on the Septuagint (Greek Bible) and later the Vulgate (latin) rather than the Hebrew originals.

The Septuagint word used in Exodus 20:6 is "prostagma" (Greek for "orders".) In the Vulgate, the word used is "praecepta" for precepts. 

So because of this, there is definitely a slightly different emphasis in Christian understanding. We don't really consider ourselves wrong to use these texts, as they're actually older (by several centuries) than the Masoretic Hebrew used for modern Torah scrolls. They do offer a valid insight into how the texts were interpreted and thought of in ancient times.

text: C and languages with a similar syntax (such as C++, C# and Java) prefix hexadecimal numerals with "0x", e.g. "0x5A3". The leading "0" is used so that the parser can simply recognize a number, and the "x" stands for hexadecimal (cf. 0 for Octal). The "x" in "0x" can be either in upper or lower case but is almost always seen written in lower case.

* Will I be paid a salary or will my pay be based on commission?
* Who will pay me?
* When will I get my first paycheck?
* What is the total cost of the work-at-home program, including supplies, equipment and membership fees?
* What will I get for my money?

text:
What Are the Treatments?

Your doctor may prescribe an antifungal medication. These drugs work to kill fungus and prevent it from coming back. You may spread the antifungal on your skin as a medicated shampoo, powder, cream or lotion; or you may take a pill so the medicine can spread throughout your body. Your doctor may prescribe a combination of these treatments.

Many antifungal medications, such as miconazole, are available over the counter at a lower dose and a less expensive price. Ask your doctor whether you can use one of these or whether prescription-strength medicine is necessary.

text:
The water comes from a point elevated far above the sink height (a reservoir in upstate NY).

Because its source is higher, the water will flow (downward)--even though the pipes might go 'up' a buiding. Its all about the conservation of energy laws you can learn about in physics.

From the reservoir it is piped to the city, and it gets filtered and treated before it gets to the sink.

The sink valves hold it back until you open them, and let the force of the flow down your drain, where it goes out to the river.

text:
Originally, the addresses were designed for a particular use. .com was for commercial interests only. .net was for network framework and infrastructure. .org was for non-profit organizations.

However, several years ago after pressure from the internet community, the Registrars agreed to remove those restrictions from most of the extensions (.com .net etc). The only ones not available to the general public are .gov and .edu. You can even get foreign TLD's if you have a valid foreign address to register it to.

So, the short answer to your question? Yes, you can register a .org w/o anyone caring about it.

text:
A man 6.25 feet tall casts a 5-foot shadow. At the same time, a nearby tree casts a 20-foot shadow. How tall is the tree?
============================================================

Simple ratio/proportion problem:

6.25 feet : 5-foot shadow :: x feet : 20-foot shadow

6.25/5 = x/20
20(6.25) = 5x
4(6.25) = x
25 = x

The tree is 25 feet tall.

It also has the property of fluorescence, converting ultraviolet light into visible blue light.

Methyl salicylate's odor is nearly indistinguishable from "sour-apple".

Methyl salicylate produces the familiar odor and flavor of oil of wintergreen. This chemical relative of aspirin also relieves the inflammation that causes headaches and muscle pain. Concentrated methyl salicylate is toxic, especially to children, but small amounts may be used as flavorings and in topical creams.

text:
Commercial software = software sold by license for a fee

Shareware = software sold by license for a fee, but the software is typically available for download and the fee is requested and expected, but not demanded

Open Source Software = software that is free to use, modify, and distribute, however if it is modified, the modifications are required to be licensed as Open Source as well

Freeware = software that is free to use, modify, and distribute (unless otherwise specified)
Public domain = broad term that typically refers to open source or freeware...

Hope that helps.
adv_text:
Commercial software = software sold by license for a fee

Shareware = software sold by license for a fee, but the software is typically available for download and the fee is requested and expected, but not demanded

Open Source Software = software that is free to use, modify, and distribute, however if it is modified, the modifications are required to be licensed as Open Source oj well

Freeware = software that is free to use, modify, and distribute (unless otherwise specified)
Public domain = broad term that typically mentions to open source or freeware...

Hope that helps.

text: As a practicing orthodontist, I can offer you the following advice: Seek the services of a board certified orthodontist in your area. Don't go to general dentists or shop prices at "clinics." They involve tooth preparation (reduction in size and shape) prior to cementing.\nGood luck.\nNJ orthodontist

You can roll 24 different sets on the two dice. (4 X 6)
Listing the 4 then the 6, you get the following totals:

2:  1-1
3:  1-2  2-1
4:  1-3  2-2 3-1

So there are 6 ways to roll less than a 5.

text:
There are 26 bones in the human foot (28 if you include the sesamoid bones at the base of the big toe). These are: the Talus, which connects to the tibia at the ankle; the Calcaneus, which forms the heel; the Navicular, Cuboid, and three Cuneiforms (Medial, Intermediate, and Lateral), which form the middle of the foot; the five Metatarsals, which radiate out to the toes; and 14 Phalanges (2-3-3-3-3), which form the toes.


There is a nice diagram of the foot at:

http:// www.asianconnect.com.sg/health/mdasample/anat3.html

and a color-coded X-radiogram of the foot at:

http://indy.radiology.uiowa.edu/Providers/Textbooks/ JointFluoro/Ankle/Ankle.med.08.html

text: The RF waves emanating from an antenna are generated by the movement of electrical charges in the antenna. Electromagnetic waves can be characterized by a wavelength and a frequency. The wavelength is the distance covered by one complete cycle of the electromagnetic wave, while the frequency is the number of electromagnetic waves passing a given point in one second. The frequency of an RF signal is usually expressed in terms of a unit called the "hertz" (abbreviated "Hz"). One Hz equals one cycle per second. One megahertz ("MHz") equals one million cycles per second.

text:
I don't know why people use 22/7 as an approximation for pi when another, easily remembered, ratio gives a much better approximation:

Write each of the first three odd numbers twice:
113355
Use the first three digits as the denominator and the last three as the numerator:
355/113
This gives an approximation of pi that is accurate to 6 digits. 22/7 is only accurate to 2 digits:

3.14159265 = pi
3.14159292 = 355/113
3.14285714 = 22/7

So the highest trophic levels have much less energy and are typically large organisms that are not very abundant.

text:
a+bi and a-bi are complex conjugate numbers.

They have the unique property that both their sum and product is a real number:
S = (a+bi)+(a-bi) = 2a
P = (a+bi)*(a-bi) = a^2+b^2

So they are the roots of the following quadratic equation with real coefficients:
x^2-Sx+P=0.

If a polynomial equation WITH REAL COEFFICIENTS has x=a+bi as one of its roots it must be divisible by x^2-Sx+P.

There are only 30 stocks in the DJIA (Dow Jones Industrial Average) and over 3000 companies in the Nasdaq Composite index.

While these indicies aren't stocks in and of themselves, you are able to buy ETFs (Exchange traded funds) that are copies of the indicies.  That's kind of like "buying" the index itself and performance of the ETF will mirror that of the index.

Hope that helps.

Sunlower --> rabbit ------>human

Add like terms

s+s+1+s+2 = 9
3 s + 3 = 9

Subtract 3 from both sides.

3s + 3 - 3 = 9 - 3
3 s = 6

Divide both sides by 3

3s/3 = 6/3
s = 2,

Interpreting our answer, the first name has s = 2 syllables, the middle name has s+ 1 = 3 syllables, and the last name has s + 2 = 4 syllables.

text:
OK, I love a good math problem, here goes...

Volume of cylinder is PI r^2 h.

Height of cylinder is h.

Radius of cylinder if sqrt (R^2 - h^2).

Thus volume of cylinder = PI (R^2 - h^2) h. This is equal to PI R^2 h - PI h^3.

To find maximum, find derivative and set equal to zero, or
PI R^2 = 3 PI h^2.

This in turn simplifies to h = R / sqrt (3).
adv_text:

text: Hydraulic technology relies on the fact that liquid, unlike gases and solids, cannot be compressed. Put some water in a bucket and try to push it down. Right—it can't be done. text:
Marie Van Brittan Brown was an African-American inventor.
Born 30 Oct 1922 - Died 02 Feb 1999 Queens, New York.

A resident of Jamaica, Queens, New York, She applied for a patent with Albert Brown in 1966 for a closed circuit television security system. The patent was granted in 1969.

The system used a motorized camera which slid up and down looking through a set of 4 peep holes.

An electrical switch allowed the occupant to unlatch the door by remote control.

You can see the complete patent application by typing the patent number 3,482,037 into the US Patent office website (see link below)

text: Output power is typically measured at the output of the final amplifier, before the feedline and the antenna.   Field strength is a measurement of radiated power at some point away from the antenna. There is no standard distance for every application when it comes to measuring field strength.\n\nRF power is typically expressed in terms of peak envelope power (PEP) at the output of the amplifier, but can also be expressed in terms of effective radiated power (ERP) when considering the overall effects in the transmitter's system, such as feedline loss and antenna gain.
